findings.title: Tier 3 target_doc can escape the spec folder before canonical merge writes
findings.expected: Tier 3 routed target documents are restricted to the canonical target allowlist and remain inside the resolved spec folder.
findings.actual: Validated Tier 3 output preserves arbitrary target_doc strings, and memory-save joins that string to the spec folder without rejecting ../ traversal before reading and merging the target file.

findings.title: Task update matching treats dependency mentions as target-task matches
findings.expected: A task update for T002 matches the checklist item's own identifier field, not incidental mentions in other checklist lines.
findings.actual: matchUpdateInPlaceLine accepts any word-boundary occurrence of T002 on a structured checklist line, so '- [ ] T003 depends on T002' is counted as another T002 match.

findings.title: Focused tests miss the two high-risk negative cases introduced by routing and merge safety
findings.expected: Regression tests cover incidental task-id mentions on other checklist items and malicious Tier 3 target_doc traversal.
findings.actual: The packet tests cover zero-match, duplicate own-task lines, and benign Tier 3 targets, but not dependency mentions or escaped target documents.
